behaviors.description: Log entries with the same trace ID are grouped together regardless of which microservice emitted them.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The core failure is correlation logic (group-by trace ID) and can be triggered with in-memory fixtures containing multiple service names. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: No OS/network/subprocess behavior is required by this behavior text. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: Not applicable. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Integration because behavior text does not require module-boundary handoff; it only states grouping correctness by trace ID across emitters.
behaviors.test_description: Call the correlation function with a mixed in-memory list of structured log objects from several microservices sharing and not sharing trace IDs; assert groups are keyed only by trace ID and include entries from all emitting services.

behaviors.description: The timeline view orders entries by timestamp, correctly handling clock skew of up to 500ms between services.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The behavior is primarily ordering/skew-compensation logic and can be validated with deterministic timestamp fixtures. REAL-INFRA: No real OS/network/subprocess dependency is stated. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: Not applicable. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Integration because the behavior text focuses on ordering correctness, not inter-module I/O boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke timeline-ordering logic with synthetic entries from multiple services where timestamps differ by <=500ms skew patterns; assert final order matches expected corrected chronology and remains stable at skew boundaries.

behaviors.description: A latency spike (p99 above 3x the baseline) is detected and flagged in the anomaly report.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This is threshold/statistics logic (baseline, p99, comparison) plus flag decision, testable with deterministic numeric datasets. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: Real network/OS is not needed to reproduce the failure mode. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: Not applicable. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Integration because behavior text does not require validating storage/transport boundaries, only detection rule correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Feed controlled latency samples to anomaly-detection logic where computed p99 is below, at, and above 3x baseline; assert only >3x cases are flagged and surfaced in anomaly report payload.

behaviors.description: Malformed JSON log entries are skipped without crashing the pipeline, and the count of skipped entries is reported.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Real failure boundary is parser + pipeline control-flow + reporting counter handoff; a pure parser unit test would miss 'pipeline keeps running and reports skipped count'. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) No required real OS/network/subprocess in text, so not System. (b) No AI/ML output dependency, so not Agentic. (c,d) No agentic chaining/orchestration. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected System because behavior text does not require real file/socket/process input; in-process module wiring is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process pipeline test with a mixed batch of valid and malformed JSON entries; assert processing completes (no crash), valid entries continue through downstream stages, and skipped-entry metric/report equals malformed count.

behaviors.description: The tool handles a burst of 10,000 log entries per second without dropping entries or falling behind.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: Throughput/backpressure and 'falling behind' are runtime/timing behaviors that depend on real process scheduling, queues, and I/O characteristics; in-process fakes can hide these failures (MOCK-HIDE). MOCK-MISS: Cross-module interaction under load is central, but Integration alone may miss real performance collapse. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: Not applicable. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires burst-rate handling (10,000/sec), a real infrastructure performance boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a load test against the real tool process using a real input stream/source generating 10,000 structured logs/sec for a sustained window; assert ingest count equals sent count, processing lag stays within defined bound, and no drop/overflow indicators occur.
